text:
Once upon an autumn afternoon, young Leo came stumbling home late after finding books about owls. His grandpa noticed him creeping up the stairs. With a cheeky smile, he said, "You know you've found our secret. Any owl stuff would do."

So Grandpa fixed owl pellet molds. They experimented together. Leo found tiny feathers. They made owl feathers hats. Friends came. They owl-hatted.

One night, owls cheeted late. Just like nocturnal friends. Leo slept late. Birds stole his feather friends. He searched. Found owl pellets. Came real owl stuff. Had story time. Owls think I'm silly. But I'm their bookworm now.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
